etymology.description: semantic 髟 ("hair") + phonetic 松 (sōng) -> meaning
etymology.interpretation: 鬆(鬆) (sōng, "loose") combines 髟(髟) (biāo, "hair") as the semantic element expressing the idea of looseness or something flowing freely, similar to hair, with 松(松) (sōng, "pine") as the phonetic element. This links to the concept of being loose or relaxed, as hair can freely flow and pine branches are not tightly bound.
etymology.simplification: The simplified form 松(鬆) (sōng, "pine") retains 毛(毛) (máo, "fur") as a reduced element replacing 髟(髟) (biāo, "hair") and removing excess components for simplicity.

examples.chinese: 院子里有一棵高大的松树(院子裡有一棵高大的鬆樹)。
examples.pinyin: Yuànzi lǐ yǒu yī kē gāodà de sōngshù.
examples.english: There is a tall pine tree in the yard.

examples.chinese: 那片森林以松树闻名(那片森林以鬆樹聞名)。
examples.pinyin: Nà piàn sēnlín yǐ sōngshù wénmíng.
examples.english: That forest is famous for its pine trees.

examples.chinese: 他躺在松针覆盖的地面上(他躺在鬆針覆蓋的地面上)。
examples.pinyin: Tā tǎng zài sōngzhēn fùgài de dìmiàn shàng.
examples.english: He lay on the ground covered with pine needles.
